_comment: Manual SCR checks — mirrors Brendon's 'Pentesting Notes/Source Code Review/Manual Testing' markdown. Each rule greps the codebase the way you would by hand after the automated scans. Edit freely: add/remove rules or tune severity. 'kind' is 'regex' or 'literal'; 'scope' limits by file group (code|web|config|all); 'flags' may include 'i' (case-insensitive) and 'm' (multiline, . matches newlines). Noisy patterns are given low base severity + low confidence so the triage pass flags them for quick manual confirmation.

rules.title: Use of eval() — possible code/command injection
rules.description: eval() call found. If user-controllable input reaches this eval without special-character validation/sanitisation, it enables code or command injection.

rules.title: Hardcoded AWS Access Key ID
rules.description: A string matching the AWS Access Key ID format (AKIA + 16 uppercase alphanumerics) is hardcoded in the source.

rules.title: Hardcoded JWT token
rules.description: A three-part base64url string beginning with 'ey' (a JWT header) is hardcoded. Hardcoded JWTs often embed credentials or grant standing access.

rules.title: Possible hardcoded password (assignment)
rules.description: A password-like identifier is assigned a string literal. Confirm it is a real credential rather than a variable name, placeholder, or empty default.

rules.title: 'password' keyword occurrence
rules.description: Occurrence of the word 'password' — broad sweep per manual notes. Review surrounding code for hardcoded values or insecure handling. High-noise check; expect many benign hits.

rules.title: Email address in source (.com)
rules.description: An email address ending in .com is present in the source. May indicate a hardcoded account, internal contact, or test credential.

rules.title: Block comment (/* ... */)
rules.description: C-style block comment. Developer comments sometimes leak credentials, TODOs, disabled security controls, internal URLs, or design notes worth reviewing.

rules.title: Hardcoded Windows path (C:\)
rules.description: A hardcoded C:\ Windows path was found. Hardcoded absolute paths break portability and can leak environment/deployment details.

rules.title: Hardcoded Windows path (D:\)
rules.description: A hardcoded D:\ Windows path was found. Hardcoded absolute paths break portability and can leak environment/deployment details.

rules.title: Use of MD5
rules.description: MD5 referenced. If used for password storage, signatures, or integrity, it is cryptographically broken — prefer SHA-256+ (or bcrypt/argon2 for passwords). Non-security checksum use (e.g. cache keys) is a false positive.

rules.title: Possible insecure randomness
rules.description: A general-purpose random() call was found. If the value is security-relevant (token, password, session id, nonce), use a CSPRNG (SecureRandom / secrets / crypto.randomBytes) instead. UI/sampling use is a false positive.

rules.title: SSLConnectionSocketFactory usage — check TLS version
rules.description: SSLConnectionSocketFactory found. Confirm an explicit modern TLS protocol (TLSv1.2/1.3) is set and hostname/cert verification is not disabled.

rules.title: SSL reference — verify TLS version pinned
rules.description: An SSL reference was found. Ensure deprecated SSLv2/SSLv3 are not used and that a specific TLS version is defined. Broad check; verify manually.

rules.title: Cleartext HTTP URL
rules.description: A plaintext http:// URL was found (schemas / w3.org / localhost excluded). Data sent over HTTP is unencrypted; use https://.

rules.title: Stack trace handling — possible sensitive error exposure
rules.description: Stack trace reference found. If a stack trace is returned to the client or logged with sensitive data, it can disclose internal application details. Confirm it is not surfaced to end users.

rules.title: Possible SQL Injection — query built by string concatenation
rules.description: A SQL keyword string appears to be concatenated with a variable. If user input reaches this query without parameterisation, it is SQL injection. Confirm the input source and switch to parameterised queries / an ORM.

rules.title: Possible SQL Injection — dynamic query passed to execute()
rules.description: A query executed via execute()/rawQuery()/query() is built with interpolation/concatenation (f-string, +, .format, ${}). Verify whether user input reaches it; use bound parameters instead.

rules.title: Possible DOM XSS sink (innerHTML / document.write)
rules.description: A DOM sink that renders HTML was found. If attacker-controllable data reaches it without encoding/sanitisation (e.g. DOMPurify), it is DOM-based XSS. Prefer textContent or sanitised output.

rules.title: Framework XSS bypass (dangerouslySetInnerHTML / v-html / bypassSecurityTrust)
rules.description: A framework's built-in output encoding is being bypassed to render raw HTML. Confirm the value is fully sanitised; otherwise this is stored/reflected XSS.

rules.title: Possible reflected/stored XSS — output encoding disabled
rules.description: Template auto-escaping is disabled or user input is echoed unescaped (|safe, mark_safe, {{{ }}}, autoescape off, echo $_GET). Confirm the data is trusted; otherwise it is XSS.

rules.title: Possible OS Command Injection sink
rules.description: A call that executes an OS command was found (exec/system/popen/ProcessBuilder/shell=True). If user input reaches the command string without allow-list validation, it is command injection.

rules.title: Possible Path Traversal — file access from user input
rules.description: A file is opened/read/served using a path that appears to come from user input. Without canonicalisation (resolve real path + verify it stays under an allowed base), this allows reading/writing arbitrary files.

rules.title: XML parser — verify external entities are disabled (XXE)
rules.description: An XML parser was found. Unless external entities/DTDs are explicitly disabled (e.g. disallow-doctype-decl, FEATURE_SECURE_PROCESSING, resolve_entities=False, libxml_disable_entity_loader), user-supplied XML enables XXE (file read, SSRF, DoS).

rules.title: Possible Server-Side Template Injection (SSTI)
rules.description: A template is compiled/rendered from a dynamic string. If user input is part of the template (not just the data), it enables SSTI, frequently escalating to remote code execution. Pass user data as template variables, never as template text.

rules.title: Insecure Deserialization sink
rules.description: An unsafe deserialisation API was found (pickle, yaml.load, ObjectInputStream/readObject, unserialize, Marshal.load, BinaryFormatter, TypeNameHandling, node-serialize). If the serialized data crosses a trust boundary, an attacker can achieve code execution or object injection. Use safe formats (JSON) or safe loaders.

rules.title: Permissive CORS policy (wildcard origin)
rules.description: A wildcard/any Cross-Origin policy was found. Combined with Allow-Credentials, this lets malicious sites read authenticated responses. Restrict to an explicit allow-list of trusted origins.

rules.title: CORS Allow-Credentials enabled — verify origin is not wildcard
rules.description: Allow-Credentials:true was found. This must never be combined with a wildcard or reflected origin, or any site can make authenticated cross-origin requests and read the responses.

rules.title: Weak clickjacking protection (X-Frame-Options / frame-ancestors)
rules.description: Framing protection is disabled or permissive (ALLOWALL, ALLOW-FROM, frame-ancestors *, frameguard:false). Set X-Frame-Options: DENY/SAMEORIGIN or a strict CSP frame-ancestors to prevent clickjacking.

rules.title: CSRF protection explicitly disabled
rules.description: CSRF protection is turned off (csrf_exempt, csrf.disable(), WTF_CSRF_ENABLED=False, VerifyCsrfToken $except). Any cookie-authenticated state-changing endpoint left unprotected is vulnerable to CSRF.

rules.title: State-changing endpoint (POST/PUT/DELETE) — verify CSRF token
rules.description: A state-changing route was found. If it is cookie/session authenticated, confirm a CSRF token is required and validated. Pure bearer-token APIs are generally exempt. Manual verification pointer.

rules.title: Possible IDOR — object fetched by user-supplied id
rules.description: A record is looked up using an identifier taken directly from the request. Confirm the handler verifies the current user owns / is authorised for that object before returning it; otherwise it is an IDOR / broken access control.

rules.title: Privileged/admin route — verify authorisation enforced
rules.description: An admin/internal/management route path was found. Confirm strong server-side authorisation (role/permission check) is enforced on it and it is not reachable by lower-privileged or anonymous users.

rules.title: Authentication endpoint — verify rate limiting / lockout
rules.description: A login/authentication endpoint was found. Confirm it enforces rate limiting / account lockout / CAPTCHA against brute-force and credential-stuffing, and that failures do not leak whether the account exists. Manual verification pointer.

rules.title: Possible user enumeration — account-specific auth error
rules.description: An error message distinguishes 'user not found' from 'wrong password'. This lets an attacker enumerate valid accounts. Return a single generic message (e.g. 'invalid username or password') for all authentication failures.

rules.title: File upload handler — verify extension/content-type allow-list
rules.description: A file-upload handler was found. Confirm it validates the file server-side by an allow-list of extensions AND content-type/magic bytes, renames the stored file, caps size, and stores it outside any web-executable path. Otherwise an attacker may upload a web shell (arbitrary extension) for RCE.

rules.title: Possible Open Redirect — redirect target from user input
rules.description: A redirect uses a destination that appears to come from user input. Without an allow-list / relative-only check, an attacker can redirect users to a malicious site (phishing, OAuth token theft). Validate the target against known-safe URLs.

rules.title: Possible SSRF — outbound request to user-supplied URL
rules.description: An outbound HTTP request uses a URL/host that appears user-controllable. Without an allow-list, an attacker can reach internal services or cloud metadata (169.254.169.254). Enforce an allow-list and block internal/link-local ranges.

rules.title: Insecure cookie flags (HttpOnly/Secure/SameSite)
rules.description: A cookie is set with HttpOnly/Secure disabled or SameSite=None. Session/auth cookies must be HttpOnly + Secure + SameSite=Lax/Strict to resist theft via XSS and interception. Confirm this cookie carries sensitive data.

rules.title: Debug / development mode enabled
rules.description: Debug or development mode appears enabled. In production this exposes stack traces, interactive debuggers (e.g. Werkzeug console = RCE), and verbose errors. Ensure debug is disabled in production configuration.

rules.title: TLS certificate validation disabled
rules.description: TLS/certificate verification is turned off (verify=False, rejectUnauthorized:false, InsecureSkipVerify:true, VERIFYPEER 0, trust-all callback). This allows man-in-the-middle interception of 'encrypted' traffic. Never disable verification in production.

rules.title: Possible LDAP Injection — filter built from input
rules.description: An LDAP filter appears to be assembled from concatenated/interpolated input. If user data reaches the filter unescaped, an attacker can alter the query. Escape LDAP special characters or use parameterised filters.

rules.title: JWT signature not verified / alg:none
rules.description: A JWT appears to be decoded without signature verification or allows the 'none' algorithm. This lets an attacker forge arbitrary tokens. Always verify with a fixed algorithm and secret/public key.

rules.title: Possible Singapore NRIC/FIN in source
rules.description: A token matching the Singapore NRIC/FIN format (S/T/F/G/M + 7 digits + checksum letter) is present. Real personal identifiers must never be embedded in code or test fixtures; confirm it is not live PII.

rules.title: Possible credit-card number (PAN) in source
rules.description: A digit sequence matching a Visa/Mastercard/Amex/Discover PAN pattern is present. PCI-DSS prohibits storing cardholder data in source; confirm it is not a live PAN (may be a test card).

rules.title: Private key material committed to source
rules.description: A PEM private-key header is present in the repository. Private keys must never be committed; rotate the key and load it from a secure store at runtime.

rules.title: Use of a weak/broken symmetric cipher (DES/RC4/Blowfish)
rules.description: A weak or broken symmetric cipher is referenced. DES/3DES/RC4/Blowfish are cryptographically weak; use AES-GCM or ChaCha20-Poly1305.

rules.title: Insecure ECB cipher mode
rules.description: ECB block-cipher mode leaks plaintext structure (identical blocks encrypt identically). Use an authenticated mode such as GCM.

rules.title: Unsafe YAML deserialization
rules.description: YAML is loaded without a safe loader (yaml.load without SafeLoader, or SnakeYAML's default constructor), which can instantiate arbitrary types from untrusted input. Use yaml.safe_load / a SafeConstructor.

rules.title: Possible prototype pollution sink (__proto__ write)
rules.description: Assignment through __proto__ / constructor.prototype can allow prototype pollution if the key/value is user-controllable. Confirm the property path is not attacker-influenced.

rules.title: Possible XPath injection sink
rules.description: An XPath expression is evaluated. If user input is concatenated into the expression without escaping, it enables XPath injection.

rules.title: Hardcoded JWT signing secret
rules.description: A JWT is signed with, or a signing key is assigned from, a hardcoded literal. Anyone with the source can forge valid tokens. Load the signing key from a secret store.

rules.title: Spring Boot Actuator endpoints broadly exposed
rules.description: Actuator endpoints are exposed with a wildcard or include sensitive endpoints (env, heapdump). These can leak configuration/secrets or allow heap capture if unauthenticated.

rules.title: Insecure Electron webPreferences
rules.description: nodeIntegration:true or contextIsolation:false exposes Node APIs to renderer content, turning any XSS into remote code execution. Enable contextIsolation and disable nodeIntegration.

rules.title: postMessage to wildcard target origin
rules.description: window.postMessage is called with '*' as targetOrigin, allowing any origin to receive the message. Specify an explicit target origin; also validate event.origin on the receiver.
